doc.title: On credit, both chairs — AR and AP lifecycles
doc.event: Same $900 job, two chairs. As the SELLER: the studio finishes work and collects 30 days later. As the BUYER: the studio receives a $900 supply order and pays 30 days later. Journalize each chair's two-entry lifecycle.

lines.label: claim is born
lines.why: An IOU in your favor — an asset from the moment the work is done.
lines.trap: AR is created by EARNING, never by collecting.

lines.label: earned now
lines.trap: This is the only revenue this job will EVER record — remember that when the cash shows up.

lines.label: asset form changes
lines.trap: Total assets unchanged: one asset swapped for another.

lines.label: claim dies
lines.why: The IOU is settled and leaves the books.
lines.trap: NO revenue here — crediting Service Revenue on collection double-counts the job. Most-made error in the chapter.

lines.label: asset arrives
lines.why: You HAVE the supplies now — ownership, not payment, puts them on your books.
lines.trap: Supplies are an asset until USED (using them up is an adjusting entry — next chapter).

lines.label: IOU against you
lines.why: The mirror of the seller's AR — their receivable is your payable.
lines.trap: One transaction, two sets of books, perfectly mirrored. Accounting is a two-player game.

lines.label: IOU settled
lines.trap: NOT Supplies Expense — the supplies were recorded at delivery; paying the bill is pure settlement.

lines.label: cash out
lines.why: Both sides of the equation shrink together: A −900, L −900. Still balanced.
lines.trap: Paying a liability is never an expense — the cost (if any) was counted when incurred.

memorize.body: Both lifecycles are two entries: BIRTH (earn→AR+Revenue, or receive→Asset/Expense+AP) then SETTLEMENT (Cash↔AR, or AP↔Cash). Settlement never touches income.
Your AR is someone's AP — every credit sale is a mirror-image pair of entries on two sets of books.

questions.prompt: A client pays the $900 they've owed for 30 days (the job was recorded when finished). Today's credit is…

distractors.feedback: Crediting Service Revenue today would record the job twice. The revenue lived in the FIRST entry; today is Dr Cash 900 / Cr Accounts Receivable 900 — an asset swap.

build.scaffold: Name the chair, then the phase: birth records the economics; settlement just moves cash against the IOU. If income appears in a settlement entry, stop.
